_help._note: Allowed values + requirements per field (this _help block is ignored by the gate — keep or delete). Run `plumb schema` for the full reference.
_help.task_id: required — ticket/issue/branch id (also the receipt filename)
_help.agent_id: required — which agent or human did the work
_help.intent: required — what + why, plain language — the semantic review reads this
_help.self_modifying: one of: true | false — required — MUST be true if changed_files touch policy.protected_paths; touching one with false is a hard fail; true routes to human review
_help.validation_plan: required — each: { command, reason, required, id?, ci_covered? }
_help.validation_plan[].required: one of: true | false — required — is this check mandatory
_help.validation_plan[].id: optional — optional step id; evidence is matched to it via execution_evidence[].step (robust to a command wording diff)
_help.validation_plan[].ci_covered: one of: true | false — optional — step is corroborated by the ci-evidence gate (real CI run), not manual evidence — may be 'skipped'; also auto-recognized when command matches a policy ci_evidence_checks entry
_help.execution_evidence: required — each: { command, status, output_ref?, skip_reason?, step? }
_help.execution_evidence[].status: one of: passed | failed | skipped — required — required steps must be 'passed' (unless CI-covered); use skip_reason when 'skipped'
_help.execution_evidence[].step: optional — optional id of the validation_plan step this evidence is for (matches validation_plan[].id)
_help.changed_files: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — don't hand-edit
_help.base_sha: optional — pinned merge-base the diff was computed against — makes gate verification deterministic; set by `plumb receipt --write` — never hand-edit
_help.diff_sha256: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — never hand-edit (computed from base_sha)
_help.result_summary: required — what changed + how it was verified

intent: Add a phased (fail-cheap-first) CI gate: `plumb run` gains a `--phase quality|verify|full` flag (#58, v0.6.0). `quality` runs shape + semantic review only and SKIPS ci-evidence (tests haven't run yet), so an agent's iterate loop spins against the ~2-min cheap phase; a REWORK fails fast so the workflow `needs:` chain blocks the expensive test phase, while a clean phase-1 exits 0. `verify` runs the ci-evidence gate + the terminal PASS/REWORK/REVIEW verdict, assuming phase 1 passed. `full` (default) is today's unchanged all-in-one gate, so non-staged consumers are untouched (back-compat). The shape/semantic/ci-evidence gate LOGIC is unchanged — only WHEN each runs, exit codes, and phase-provenance verdict wording (a phase-1 result reads as 'tests SKIPPED', never 'tests passed').

validation_plan.reason: Full test suite (274 tests) incl. 4 new cli-phase.test.ts cases proving quality skips ci-evidence, verify/full run it, and an unknown --phase exits 2. Bound to the `test` ci-evidence check.
tsc must compile the new --phase branching + action.yml passthrough with no type errors.
The Action runs dist/index.js; rebuild the bundle so it doesn't drift from src/ (dist-check CI job).

execution_evidence.output_ref: # tests 274 # pass 274 # fail 0 (incl. 4 new: quality skips ci-evidence; verify/full attempt it; bogus phase exit 2)

result_summary: Shipped the v0.6.0 phased gate: `plumb run --phase quality|verify|full` (default full = unchanged), gated on a single `runCiEvidence = phase !== 'quality'` hinge so quality (phase 1) skips ci-evidence and verify/full run it. Added phase-provenance reasons that make a phase-1 result read as 'tests SKIPPED, agent-fixable', an unknown-phase exit-2 guard, a `phase` input on action.yml threaded to the CLI, templates/workflow-staged.yml (quality → tests needs:[quality] → gate needs:[tests] with checks/issues:write), README + CHANGELOG docs, and version bump to 0.6.0. Verified: npm run build (tsc clean), npm test (274/274 incl. 4 new cli-phase cases), npm run bundle (dist/index.js rebuilt so the Action bundle matches src/). self_modifying:true — action.yml is a protected path, so this routes to human review.
